reviews.review_text: My playtime of 17k+ hours speak for themself. Amazing game design. Endless replayability and endless mods make this my favorite game of all times.

Decided to start a normal, peaceful game. 1 hour later I was selling organs to caravans.
11/10

rimworld is an awesome game designed so that even if you "fail" (your entire colony dies) its treated as an opportunity for another colony/story to begin but i JUST cant resist saving and loading every 5 minutes so i dont lose my progress and loading my last save file after a colonist gets shot in the brain and dies by a lancer again. it was 2.1% for it to hit i think that was justified my one and only rimworld

Crazy, fun and sometimes unexpected colony sim. Every playthrough is very different and there's a LOT to explore and experience in the game. Would definitely recommend it!


Some quality of life mods are highly recommended and you can go nuts with what the mod community has done so far.

RimWorld is one of the most unique and addictive colony simulation games I’ve ever played. It takes a simple idea—surviving on a distant planet—and turns it into an endlessly complex storytelling experience.

The gameplay is deep, flexible, and constantly engaging. Managing resources, building your colony, and keeping your colonists alive creates constant tension, especially when random events throw everything into chaos.

One of the best parts of RimWorld is how every playthrough tells its own story. The AI storyteller system generates unpredictable events that lead to moments that can be dramatic, funny, or completely heartbreaking.

The freedom it gives you is also a huge highlight. You can build your colony however you want, experiment with different strategies, and approach problems in multiple ways, which makes every run feel different.

Overall, RimWorld is a masterpiece of simulation and emergent storytelling. It’s chaotic, deep, and endlessly replayable, easily earning its place as one of the best games in its genre. 

Gameplay: 10/10 Masterpiece | Overall: 10/10 Masterpiece

Despite the developers insistence its a story generator it really isn't.

It's more like rng hell mixed with colony tycoon which Isn't bad but gets boring very fast. Scenarios begin to repeat quite quickly.
Have over 2000 hours on this now and no playthrough is ever the same. The Mods for this game are second to none.  Great Game

What more is there to say that hasn't been said? This is the apex of the colony sim genre, not just for its accessibility, intuitiveness, and robust out-of-the-box content offering, but for its vast workshop community and flexible modding framework that turns the experience into pretty much any damn thing you want it to be as long as it involves moving a living being around a planet. All gamers with an interest in strategy and simulation owe it to themselves to check out the indie titan that triumphantly brought the Dwarf Fortress formula into the mainstream.

To everyone who has ever worked on Rimworld or its mods, I salute you. It's pure experience-first gaming of the highest quality. A truly one-of-a-kind piece of media.
its like jerking off but better it is very addictive game to play
This game will run well on a potato with full memory. Be very careful - it is VERY VERY VERY addictive
Cool game but I gotta spend more then the gross domestic product of the democratic republic of the Congo just for DLC’s that all the good mods rely on

One of my earlier colonies in this game consisted of a doctor researcher who would research prosthetic organs so that he could implant a prosthetic heart on his girlfriend who was suffering from organ decay. He did the surgery on her. It went well. He married her after.

Well, the game is not horrible, but it certainly doesn't qualify to be recommended when far better alternatives exist. Too much low-effort positive feedback exists that clouds the rational, realistic image of the game.

I just completed the game by lifting off 54 colonists into space, and, well... I feel a bit disappointed. I've played a few other games (very) similar to RimWorld, and this one simply underperforms.

It feels like interracting with the world serves no purpose. It's the endless grind of cleaning animal 💩, refrigerating food, killing waves of enemies you hope to recruit or harvest the organs from --- and that's all. Destroying enemy villages and outposts results in nothing. Eliminating their leaders results in nothing as well. Such little choices make huge impacts in games like Kenshi where eliminating an enemy outposts creates a power vacuum and result in the ultimate dominion of another enemy entity in the region, and killing a leader collapses an entire faction and makes interesting changes. RimWorld feels dead. You feel isolated. There is nothing to explore, there's no risk you can take by making a bad or risky move... [b]It's only the bad RNG that really presents a threat.[/b] It's essentially a game of rolling a dice giving an illusion that you're accomplishing something.

There isn't a single reason why you should send trade caravans anywhere. Once you research underground drills and underground scanners, you don't even need to trade for metals. The few components and high tech components you lack and could trade for --- you need them in far greater quantity than what the trade offers, and you don't actually lack materials for crafting the components yourself either... You just have 90% of your workforce cleaning animal 💩, trash and blood off the floors to prevent another dumb mental breakdown, micromanaging workers to clean the kitchen (which they refuse and don't prioritize for some reason) to prevent insanity-inducing food poisoning --- so no colonist can truly commit to crafting.

Trading food is pointless when everything perishes rapidly aside from packed survival meal and pemmican. I do not understand why the global map even exists when nothing you do on the map makes any difference, and it pretty much doesn't affect your tiny piece of the map either way. The worldmap is there to give you an illusion of spaciousness in a truely claustrophobic game.

The colonist happiness is also an utter load of 💩.
Yeah, sure, your colonist slept in his own clean room with a masterwork marble bed with a marble wardrobe and marble end table with masterwork jade art, yeah he ate a lavish meal, ate some chocolate, had one drink, had billiard to play, huge TV to watch, books to read, he was in a clean environment, he was healthy and had good relations with almost everyone --- but there, I had to recruit him in his top-tier plaststeel armor to defend against a few mad emus attacking the base.

What's that?

He decided to have a random mental breakdown and instead try to kill another colonist that's also defending the base with 40 emus closing on our 15-men line? Ffffff offfffffff!!!

The constant unhappiness is why sending search / loot parties on the worldmap makes no sense, aside from the fact there's no purpose in exploring because you can't find anything interesting or game-changing (which is why they probably made it so punishing in the first place). However, if you still try to go to another area to establish a camp and mine some ores, you'll be dealing with 80% of your colonists acting like North American ADHD kids having mental breakdowns: attacking your own people, lighting fires, wandering away, falling into catatonia. The length of the expedition (hint: it's always very short) doesn't depend on food, safety, weather... It depends on parenting a few poorly scripted burdens you call colonists. Not to mention you draw critical manpower from the main base and leave it vulnerable to ganking enemy attacks on higher difficulty...

... and speaking of enemy attacks on higher difficulty if you hope for a little bit better challenge than shooting fish in a barrel ...

The enemy attacks are absurd and the AI is utterly horrible. If you have 30 colonists and choose a more difficult setting: the enemies may attack you with 60... But they do so by attempting to bumrush you with 25 shielded melee units that you cannot repel if you do not form a full-force firing line --- and non-tribal / non-mech enemies always have at least 4 doomsday rocket launcher they fire into your and into their own units because:
1) They do not care if they spend a rare single-use item, it's not like their village will become unguarded or suffer any penalties.
2) They do not care if 95% of their units perish to friendly fire because it affects their faction in no way whatsoever.
Either way, one doomsday rocket always finds a way and hits your line, killing at least 1/3 of your people and critically wounding at least another 1/3. No tactics, no interesting maneuvers, just pure brainless suicidal bruteforce --- each and every time. Spacing your units (or making layered defensive lines) makes no sense either as, for some reason, units with high level shooting still most commonly shoot and eliminate friendlies.

Sentries serve no tactical purpose as well, they are just extra cannon fodder to slow the enemies down while you hope RNG gives an outcome where an enemy doomsday rocket misses and kills more of their own units than yours. Mortars serve very little purpose as maps are too tiny even at max size to make any difference. Mortars are only there to serve as counter-batteries against sieges and to provoke psychic drone mechs to attack your base after eliminating the drone with a barrage.

The game feels like a slot machine. You sit there and watch the screen, just waiting for another RNG popup.
The game is not completely horrible, but the quantity and quality of the content [b] DOES NOT JUSTIFY THE PRICE TAG[/b], and I'm not even mentioning the stupidly overpriced DLC.

People who first time experience this genre may find it interesting, but this is just the hyped-up lazy work from a dev uninspired to do better because he had luck selling a half-assed product.
Steep learning curve. Once learned it is a personal RP world simulator. Worth the investment. Easy to play difficult to master.

Rimworld without DLCs is an interesting thing to try to review. When I first played Rimworld, the only DLC that existed was Royalty, which I consider to be almost a must-have due to adding many things that are well enough integrated that most people who have it probably think most of it's content were in the vanilla experience.

Still, Rimworld without any DLCs is a fun game. You can settle in a variety of biomes, which have variable challenges. You can build up a colony however you want. If you want a village style colony, you can do it. Want a mountain base? You can do it. Want a giant self constrained megastructure? That's doable too. The colony building is fun. You can if you want make killboxes to easily defeat raiders. You can paint your colony structures different colors if you want. The actual colony building aspect is pretty neat even though the game lacks z levels, meaning everything is built on a flat single story plane.

The simulation aspects of colonists is pretty neat as well. The game keeps track of injuries and illnesses on each body part down to individual fingers and toes. The game also has a system that keeps track of addiction and tolerance levels to different drugs. Colonists can have individual quirks, such as being especially prone to mental breaks, or having a tendency to start fires for thrills. The game also keeps track of each colonist's opinion of others, meaning they will make friends and enemies. You might have someone have a mental breakdown because another colonist keeps bullying him for example. 

Combat is... A little simplistic. It's functional. There's a cover system. Weapons have different optimal ranges and damage types. The game keeps track of how much pain a character is feeling, causing them to keel over if their pain is too high. Individual body parts have their own health values, so characters usually die either from an organ being too damaged, or from blood loss. 

The game only gets better with DLCs. Royalty adds a whole heap of things, including psychic powers and royal titles.  Ideology lets you do themed challenge runs, such as playing as nudist vegetarians. Biotech adds children, human subspecies, and controllable mechanoids (robots basically). Anomaly adds a weird variety of horror themed content that's well described on it's steam page. Odyssey adds ships that are basically mobile bases, as well as some other features. I'd recommend them in this order:
Royalty
Biotech
Ideology
Anomaly
Odyssey

Overall, while I recommend getting at least one DLC. This game without DLC is still an easy recommend. It's probably still better than most other good colony sims such as Ratopia, Mind Over Magic, and Stranded: Alien Dawn. Although I'd still recommend those games as well. 

That said it's a bit pricy for an indie game and while I'd argue it's worth the price, that's a decision that needs to be weighed by how much you like colony sims.

Really fun and unique gameplay mechanics. I’ve enjoyed Rimworld across multiple playthroughs thanks to its incredibly high replay value. You can approach the game in countless ways. Standard colony run? Absolutely. Lone survivor story? Totally possible. Organized organ-harvesting cult? Yup, the game even supports that kind of dark creativity.

What makes Rimworld special is how it scratches that very specific itch in your gamer brain, the satisfaction of seeing long term progress unfold from your decisions. Every choice matters, and watching your colony evolve over time is endlessly rewarding.

The modding community also deserves a shoutout: it adds insane depth and variety, keeping the game fresh even after hundreds of hours. Whether you want quality-of-life tweaks or complete overhauls, there’s something for everyone.

Verdict: Rimworld is one of those rare games that feels like a true sandbox for storytelling, strategy, and experimentation. If you enjoy games that let you craft your own narrative and challenge your creativity, this is a must-play.

There are a lot of mechanics, unwritten rules, and quirks that you will have to learn about in your first several hours. Most of the time you learn this the hard way. Sometimes its a bit discouraging or annoying. But if you stick to it and improve on you past failures, this game becomes very rewarding. It is so satisfying to transform from a lowly group of a few tribespeople to an advanced civilization bent on destroying anyone who crosses you.
Quiet possibly one of the greatest games ever created. It's in my top 5 for sure.

This game is very good to say the least.
Easy to learn
Game loop is addictingly well executed
One of the biggest modding communities
Well optimised
DLCs aren't required for a proper game experience, but enrich it massively
Its really fun and challenging, I would definitely recommend to play this game and figure out how to play and survive on a futuristic or neolithic world.
best colony builder you will ever play and in case you do not like the base gameplay, there is literally 1,000,000 different mods to change that

If you want a game where everything is fine and you plant some things and tame some animals and then you get attacked by rabid Yorkshire Terriers, your crops get blight, and everything catches on fire then this game is for you!
This game is amazing. However, I would recommend much higher pc specs, because you will be adding something like 700 mods.

This game is incredibly detailed and has infinite replay value. The AI storyteller will give you a unique experience every playthrough. The base game can give you hundreds of hours, and the DLC's add entire new aspecs to the game which gives you hundreds and hundreds more. The world is so detailed and your colonists have things like emotions, interests, medical menu and history, and family, all which are unique to them. There is a reason the average playtime is a couple hundred hours for this game.

Rimworld is one of the best games that I have ever played. It has excellent developer support with updates, bug fixes, or a new expansion coming out every year. It has extensive modding support and one of the most robust modding communities even which the developers at Ludeon recognize and work with the make the game even better. The wide range of stories the game can tell as well with its base three storytellers, and the many modded ones as well. You can be a space pirate in your Gravship jumping around raiding enemies bases or make an iron fortress that could repel any enemies in the game. You can have greek tragedies casually happen before the storyteller send you a cute dog or the worst enemy raid you've faced. There is nothing like Rimworld, I doubt there will ever be like it.

This game being 35 dollars is genuinely a steal. I've sunk 200+ hours into this so far, (making it my most played steam game by far) a majority of those being on one vanilla save. 
The game gives you almost unlimited freedom to do whatever you want, and even death  is a new opportunity, as well as actually beating the game. 
I'm normally not into real-time-strategy games, yet this one is probably one of my favorite games ever, for a reason. 
In addition to what's already possible, the game has multiple DLCs, and nearly unlimited mods, with both being added on to constantly. 
Probably the best strategy game ever made. Entirely worth the 35 dollars.
I like everything about this game. Except maybe the name. If you tell people to buy Rimworld, they start treating you like some sort of sex pervert. "Oh you, like rimming do you?" Sjeesh

The base game is already great, DLCs make it even better, mods skyrocket it into what I personally think is the best game possible.

It's only downside is part of why it's so good, the fact that graphics are very simplistic. This however, makes modding a lot easier since there's no need for complicated 3D assets/texturing. Basically anyone can mod and create visuals for this game with a little time spent on Photoshop or other similar software. Even Paint if you want your mod to have that kind of style.

Anyway, there's a reason I have a ridiculous amount of hours on this game. It's good. Get it.

Great game!!
Best chaos simulator with a sprinkle of pyromania included.
Very great game, it's not just a game, it's a story teller and colony simulator. Originally was a kickstarter and shows what can be done when indie devs are given a chance.
A person who pretends to be my friend told me that this game is just Sims for men...  the worst part is that he's not wrong.
No matter what I always come back to this game since the first time I played. No other game makes me lose track of the hours quite like RimWorld. Here's to many more crazy adventures on the rim!

this is an unforgiving game that will test your ingenuity and leak into how you play other games, I love it. this game has taken more then its fare share of my attention and if you purchase it you will have the same issue. I don't see them stopping development for this game but optimization and QOL do not seem to be on the developer's minds, that is something that seems to be left to the Modmakers and looking at the wonderful workshop that doesn't seem to cross their minds very often either. my advice is that you should try to play with the base game and only adapt when needed otherwise you spoil the adventure you could have.

The game is very challenging, but every time your colony fails you learn more you try again and do better in the next one. all the while your creating story after story (even if the story is that you forgot to assign the colonist to use the butcher table so everyone just starves, lol) and the DLC's just keep adding onto the new ways to play. I love this game! (i have 200+ hours on console as well)
A dangerously addictive game that will sink its teeth so deep into you, Rimworld is my favorite game I have ever played!
The kind of game i keep coming back to. It's a great staple to the library.

Rimworld its one of the best games I have played, and I love the massive community it has.

The only bad parts of this game are the DLC's and it's difficulty.

Yet I don't really care about the DLC's as you don't need them to play mods are free and right there. As for the DLC's being being extra content, and this being their only game I do support them having DLC's, If you ever consider buying them do it to support, there are mods out there that can replace most of them.

And it's difficulty you learn by time, and it's fun to have a game giving everything to you and yet, you're not a god, you have to actually let you slaves *cough* I mean pawns rest, and even let your actual slaves rest. Difficulty is just a obstacle to overcome when playing a game.
One of the best games ever uploaded to steam. A perfect example of what moding can do to make a game endlessly playable.

A game where you build a settlement on a different world and try to survive, only to be met with an anime girl, a covenant elite, a flood, a V.O.I.D. executive and some sort of zombie outbreak, ending everything with an all out firefight, magic and an "accidental" thermonuclear explosion.

10/10, worth every ♥♥♥♥♥♥ penny

My pawns keep dying after they (or me) do crazy things
So many ways for everyone to set themselves on fire.
Love outer space areas 
Genuinly one of the strangest games I've played on both of my accounts
10/10
w game start with 1 cut off limb raiders come and harvest my other limb

I pirated this game around covid because I was young and had no money or job. i sunk easily 200 hours in the game then. i got a job and paid for it because it was such a good game to me that i felt it deserved my money. 

10/10 would eat without table again

great game to relax on, you can play it anyway you like, and it's soo hard to get bored of it, the only downside is that it's single player by default, but you can get mods for that, and it's a little hard to learn the game at the start.

This is one of my favorite games of all time. Vanilla with no mods? So much to offer. Vanilla with mods? As much depth as you can possibly bear. 

My one complaint is that the DLC integrates all of the popular mods and monetizes them. I haven't played any of the DLC yet, but I hope to one day.

For $35, this is a game that will keep giving and giving for as long as you want it to. The mod community is stellar and will provide as much content as you want for as long as you want. You can even revert the version of the game to where your favorite mods are still compatible. 

If you're on the fence about RimWorld, let this be the comment that lets you hop over. With over 1,700 hours in the game, I can say that it will keep you entertained for as long as you want it to.
game is like meth, expensive and addictive but i just cant put it down.

**** this game dude. i love it.
**** the overpriced DLC, they're amazing.
**** the storytelling you can get from this game, its easily the best out there
**** you tynan, for blessing us with this ****

Surprised I haven't rated this yet. 

It's the best colony sim on the market, bar none. In a complete league of its own. The depth, visuals, gameplay, and modding capabilities are simply excellent. 

It is the standard by which all other colony sims will be measured, and which will fall short against.

bought the game 
first hour wtf is this ♥♥♥♥
10 hours in i started to understand it
50 hours in harvest there limbs,  organs
160 hours this is the best game ever
Fun game with an incredible story teller AI and fun and immersive gathering hunting and survival top down sim.

Decided to start a normal, peaceful game. 1 hour later I was selling organs to caravans.
11/10
Buy it NOW its a steal best aNd mot expensive game ive bought worth every penny
Fantastic game. So complex. I only play cozy games and this game taught me that death can be cozy too.

one of my favorite games ever
heres a story

once upon a time i had 2 mechanator colonists and a nice base, when suddenly, nearly all of my power went out. why you may ask? because my geothermal generators need repair. "just repair them" you may think, but of course that takes components, and where are the components? nowhere, absolutly nowhere. i ended up getting raided and all of my turrets were powered down, and this was late game so the raid was like 10-20 strong heavilly armed. all in all my pawns got slaughtered. moral of the story is, save your components or you WILL regret it.

great game i continue to play it, while SAVING MY COMPONENTS
An absolutely perfect RPG that I’ve spent many evenings with and still return to from time to time. Definitely worth it.

This game is so insanely fun it doesn't feel real. It's very well balanced when it comes to making your own story and yet never being boring, which is something that some games of this type tend to struggle with. You can replay it so many times without any mods or DLC!! 100% recommend (especially for all the masochists out there)

It has that dangerous “just one more day” loop. I get completely addicted, play nonstop for days or weeks, then finally take a break. Then, a couple of weeks later, some random colony idea pops into my head, or the devs release a DLC like Odyssey, and suddenly I’m right back on the planet like I never left.

What makes RimWorld special is that it is not really a game you “finish.” Technically, yes, you can build a spaceship and leave the planet. But honestly, that feels like something you do once or twice just to say you did it. The real game is everything that happens before that: the disasters, the stupid decisions, the emotional attachment to colonists who absolutely do not deserve it, and the completely unnecessary projects you somehow convince yourself are essential.

I have lost count of how many times I started with a serious survival plan and ended up distracted by something ridiculous. A zoo full of panthers and arctic foxes. A beautiful base with almost zero security. A colony where I spent way too much time over-testing killboxes instead of actually progressing. A “temporary” setup that somehow became the permanent base. Every colony becomes its own story, and half the fun is watching your original plan fall apart in the most entertaining way possible.

RimWorld is brutal, funny, stressful, cozy, and completely unhinged depending on the day. It lets you play as a careful planner, a war criminal, a farmer, a hotel manager, a mad scientist, an animal collector, or all of the above in the same run. The mods and DLCs make it even worse in the best way, because every time you think you are done, there is always another idea to try.

This is the kind of game where failure is not even frustrating most of the time. It just becomes part of the story. Your colony burns down? Story. Your best doctor gets food poisoning during a raid? Story. Your favorite colonist has a mental break and punches the mortar shells? Tragic, but also story.

If you like sandbox games, colony sims, storytelling, strategy, chaos, or games that somehow steal hundreds of hours without you noticing, RimWorld is absolutely worth it.

I love this game. I hate this game. I will probably uninstall it again someday. Then I will reinstall it two weeks later because I had another colony idea.
I play it as a city builder rather then engaged into fights all the time. I really love that game how I play it.

I recommend this game a lot!!! if you like games where managing a small group of people takes planning and strategy to use their full set of skill best, while exploring a decently sized world and SO MUCH potential and work you can/need to do as you go on... then this is your game. I was impressed when they mentioned that the order of events and the storyline was controlled by an AI that can be customized to the desired difficulty you want. I started playing in the easiest that recommends beginners, and it perfectly matched what I could do making sure I was constantly entertained. There is so much I can say about this game that isnt in this post, but I hope for all you nerds out there that enjoy casual but also exciting gaming like this, that you find this and enjoy it too!
Really fun very replayable, i would get rimworld biotech as the first dlc.
One of the best Colony-Sims out there for sure, especially once one dives into the absolutely GOATED modding community
Best colony sim love it to bits at times i dont even realized how much time i sink in it with mods even better you want youre favorite francaise gear for the pawn just get the mod for it.

lot of fun and freedom and ability to be creative

immerse yourself in a story you create/control and watch that shiz unfold

My dog got mauled by a lynx, and my pawns died in a 3 v 1. I'm terrible at this game, yet I'm having the best gaming experience so far. And that's just with the vanilla game. Thanks for my new addiction.

This game is INSANELY hard. You have to be smart about almost everything you do. Restarting over and over feels intended. I have a hundred hours and still haven't beaten the game without loading saves every time something bad happens. But that's the charm of the game, there's fun in figuring out things that you didn't know before. Being able to make a walk-in freezer for the first time is really fun, as you can store food without it rotting, or making turrets to help defend against raids. These are all things that make the game fun, just the figuring out is the best part. If you want a game you can sink your teeth into that allows you to go at YOUR pace, this is the game for you. 10/10, I recommend.
fun game mods are nice found it while stumbling upon Mr Samuel Streamer

Be me 3 pawns
one neolithic rider comes 
i think i can beat him
he kills everyone
repeat
10/10 game buy it

The best colony survival / management sim on the market. The only cons are that it's a little pricey, especially factoring in all its expansions, and setup can take a long time, especially with creating a custom ideology. 10/10.

Very fun game. I especially like how you can do whatever, and almost anything progresses you in the game some way or another. Great concept, executed extremely well. I play on a laptop and 9/10 times even on 3x speed little to no lag.
Great game, runs well on all my systems and is a great way to pass time.

I first played this game through a pirated version—Got Lured, Hooks, and Sinker—and got hooked because of how freely I could experiment with different playstyles, whether following the guidebooks or Geneva’s suggestions; there was always something new to discover. After that, I decided to support the developers by purchasing the official version. And here we are. I’m trying to play again without mods just to explore new things I might discover.
A slight menu/mechanics learning curve for some maybe, but overall pretty fun and chill :D

extremely fun but you kinda have to dump money into dlc's
a normal playthrough of yours WILL be extremely modded as well, because some of the base game mechanics are a bit iffy

I still discover things that I've Never Seen Before completely by chance, though that's partly because of the amazing mod community. It takes about 3 minutes to load up with my specs, for what it's worth.

it's just like crack, you start small, vanilla, no mods, etc... then you try using mods. realizes needs the DLCs for the cool mods, and then you expend money should have not.
ahhhhh my dear rimworld,

If you want a game where you can run a futuristic cannibal drug cartel, managing massive growing fields of drugs and a human organ-harvesting meat processing plant under the banner of a sick religion where your citizens literally cheer while eating human flesh, watching brutal public executions of raiders, and getting high out of their minds, this is it. Would never have known I would love this game as much a I do 10/10

This game is a real sickness, once I start I can't stop. My advice buy all the DLC.
Also after 500 hours I think I've "finished" it once.
Very fun and interesting game. The RNG factor is nice as well because it's always different and you can't predict anything.
All the new updates make this game better and better! I'm loving the new spaceship mod!

opened the game, did the tutorial, saw a "cool structure" and went to break down one of the walls. My man Johnson, who was equipped with a long bow and tribal pants, was shot down by a Mechanoid with a laser gun. I would absolutely play and do the same thing again.

With over 10k hours into this abomination, it would be hard to deny the fundamental impact this game has had on my life. It hasn't all been enjoyable but it has been an ongoing challenge. With the number of times I've cursed the author, he'll never get into heaven, but I may dream of conquering space bugs and homicidal machines on my death-bed to the exclusion of all other thoughts.

The amount of options and things you can do, and the ways in which you can do them, is just crazy. Definitely check out the mods, they take this game from a masterpiece to a god tier experience. Kudos to all the devs & mod devs!

10/10 I have to say I am addicted, the trailers have way too good voice actors, hard when you first start but once you get used to it, you start making bigger and stronger colonies, love both the trailers and games.

Bought this game to build a colony.
Three hours later my doctor was addicted to yayo, my chef got into a fistfight with a turkey, and my best soldier lost both legs after insulting a man with a wooden club.

10/10 colony management simulator. Would commit accidental war crimes again.
How to describe, beyond just saying "yes, good game" this post is made at 674hrs played, I think that speaks for itself.

Please do yourself a favor and pick up Rimworld. Admittedly, I struggled a bit while I was learning, but it was part of the fun for me. Don't let that dissuade you. If learning from your mistakes doesn't sound fun to you, then you can simply scale the difficulty back to make it more enjoyable to your playstyle. 
I spent hundreds of hours playing before I delved into the vast world of Rimworld mods. It is safe to say, mods add hundreds or even thousands more hours. 
The devs do a good job with adding more content with paid DLC, which I feel is fairly priced. However, you do not need the DLC to have a good time, but some mods do require assets from the DLC.
I put this game very close to the same level of greatness as Terraria. High praise indeed.
DLCs are pricy but base game is great! also the mods in the community are top-notch.
more hours on my seperate account, love this game and all the mods available

this is one of the best games i ever played, bought the DLC's and all. i have over 200 hours playing this amazing game. if your thinking about it, just know it was amazing for me so it might be for you.
A truly unique and fun experience every single time i open this game!

very fun and addictive. It's a good game for lower end PC's imo. I loved stellaris and HOI but my computer just isnt good enough to play those games. I've never had any performance difficulties with this game.

Love this game, so much fun to be had.  Definitely recommend it to anyone looking for a deeply engaging, very strange and at times horrifying simulator.
The depth of support for mods is incredible.  Just about everything can be (and has been) modded. 
10/10

Very fun game and lots of re-playability. My only thoughts are that I wish they could some how make the world map more meaningful but I honestly can't think of a way they can do that. The star ships helped a bit but the open world outside of your plot still feels kind of pointless. Great game nonetheless.
this game is so addictive and the modding community just makes it even better!

Well it is a great game overall if you want the short version. The things I like about it are the following
- The amount of mods is insane as you can change almost anything
- It is a high stakes game that keeps the tension high
- Great replayability
The DLCs are good and great. I dont have all but if I had to rank them it would be 
1. Biotech
2. Idealogy
3. Royalty
4. Odyssey
5. Anomaly

Once i hit 6-7 hrs play time selling all the drugs and organs i can, preforming child labor and selling slave babies has just become addicting. now hitting 68hrs and still selling slave babies. Its amazing! 10/11 Recomemended for anyone of any stature
Truly a ♥♥♥♥ game you don't see gay furry vampire mech-insect rimming without a mod.

I started a game, breeded many muffalo, sold many muffalo, tried to raid a settlement, lost and ragequit.
10/10

My first world after not playing since 2019. 
-Had 3 people. Only 1 person who was good at medicine. 
-Everyone starving, Medic only one not on the verge of a mental collapse. 
-Sent medic out hunting, Almost dies 
-No one can heal her, she dies, dog goes mad because of it
-Dog starts attacking people
-Prisoner manages to escape and kills one of my guys
-Dog kills the other
-World was only 3 hours old, best game ever

Base game without DLCs is solid. Played for about 70 hours before getting a DLC. I bought all 5 and like them a lot. People talk up mods, which are impressive but I only play with QOL ones and URMC. Big fun very like.
This game is very good. It also has surprisingly good Linux support (playing on NixOS).
